title: Charred Juicy British Lamb Shepherds Pie
description: A date-night British dish showcasing tender lamb and a crispy, cheesy crust. Inspired by traditional Yorkshire recipes.
instructions: Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, and Tobiko.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over high heat. Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the pot and set it aside.
Reduce heat to medium and sauté the sliced onion and minced garlic until softened, about 8 minutes.
Add the carrots and peas to the pot, cooking for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the red wine, beef broth, and yuzu cure to the pot, scraping up any browned bits. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until slightly reduced, about 10 minutes.
Return the lamb shoulder to the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven. Roast the lamb for 20 minutes, or until tender and flavorful.
While the lamb cooks, prepare the mashed potatoes by boiling diced potatoes in salted water until tender. Drain the potatoes and mash with milk and butter.
Remove the lamb from the oven and carefully transfer it to a serving dish. Spread the mashed potatoes over the top of the lamb, creating a crispy, cheesy crust.
Transfer the dish to the oven and bake for an additional 15-20 minutes, or until the potatoes are golden brown and the filling is hot and bubbly.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
